Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, Recall And Quash Writ Of Execution, And Return Of Property Levied Upon
Off Calendar Jan-08-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alan Sherwood, Esq. 510-409-619 specially appearing for Plaintiff.
Sam Dehbozorgi, Esq. 888-922-2367 x707 for Defendant Lila B. Reed through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces there is no timely opposition and it did not receive an email contesting the tentative ruling. Plaintiff requests to continue and a compliance hearing. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT LILA REEDY's Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Default And Default Judgment and Recall And Quash Writ Of Execution.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Grant. No timely opposition filed. The motion was timely filed and served by mail on November 13, 2019. The Court declines to consider Plaintiff's untimely opposition, which was filed on December 30, 2019, six days after the statutory deadline. (Code Civ. Proc. 1005(b).) Plaintiff's counsel admits he did not seek an extension of time until December 27, after the deadline had already passed. Opposing counsel lacked authority to extend the statutory deadline. The proper approach would have been to seek a stipulated continuance of the hearing. Counsel for Plaintiff is reminded to submit courtesy copies of all filed and lodged papers with department 302 as required by Local Rule 2.7(B).

In addition, defendant has 10 days to file the answer. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
